tax: Static Sparkmote is placed at species rank beneath Sparkmote (Fulgurant order, Air Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a clinging-static body, crawling surface discharge, and contact-shock as its harm. It is separated from its sibling the Arc Stormling by its clinging crawling surface static versus the Stormling's branching air-strikes; the two share the genus body-plan but never the branching air-strike. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: surface static charge held in a small drifting mote.
ap: Static Sparkmote stands 0.2–0.5 m glowing mote. Its silhouette is a small glowing ball of static that flattens and crawls on surfaces. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a faint blue glow clinging to walls, ropes, and gear; a continuous fizzing crackle rather than a single strike; and small hairs and dust standing up wherever it crawls. It moves with a crawling drift over dry surfaces, hopping between conductors, and shows a faint clinging blue glow. Even after dispersal it can be identified from a fizzing residue and small scorch-spots where it clung, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Static Sparkmote persists in dry charged galleries, dust shafts with static build-up, and rope-rigged passages where charge clings. It does not breed; it forms where air-pressure animates a surface-static build-up into a drifting mote, and recurs whenever the dry gallery re-charges and the mote re-forms. It sustains its mass on surface static charge, drawing in static drawn from dry charged surfaces. Range is bounded by the dry charged zone: outside that band the body bleeds to ground and fades in damp air. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a dry gallery where gear crackles and dust stands up with no storm, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Static Sparkmote is roused by contact with skin, rope, or gear and otherwise lies a faint glow clinging to a wall, easily missed. It claims a single dry charged gallery and answers intrusion with crawling onto and shocking whatever touches its surface. Toward grey-folk it is a clinging contact hazard; survey crews report that Lanternkin keep ropes dry-waxed, since a Sparkmote rides damp lines to the hand. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Static Sparkmote: a body of surface static charge held in a small drifting mote; immune to electricity and to physical attack; and a defining weakness — damp and grounding, which discharge it harmlessly. Its senses run through the medium itself: surface-charge sensing along conductors. It tolerates darkness, heat, and dryness but fails at damp and grounding. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the clinging mote and crawling discharge, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Static Sparkmote are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it shocks on contact with skin, rope, or metal; it crawls along conductors to reach prey; it draws surface static to renew itself; and it clings unseen to gear until touched. Each is a direct expression of clinging surface static charge, so it cannot, for instance, strike at range, produce mass-force, or persist in damp air. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Static Sparkmote matters because it rides ropes and gear to deliver a shock at the hand, and crews now keep lines dry-waxed. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a contact-shock hazard logged with dry charged galleries, and its presence reshapes routes through the dry charged galleries, now rigged with dry-waxed lines and insulated grips. Gear that crackles and dust that stands up in a dry gallery mark a clinging Sparkmote.
g: Static Sparkmote (Fulgur repens) is a small storm-air elemental of clinging static that crawls over surfaces and shocks on contact. Documented from the dry charged galleries where surface static builds, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a drifting mote of glowing static that clings and crawls over dry surfaces — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Where the Stormling strikes from the air, the Sparkmote crawls along walls and gear, shocking whatever it touches. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a fading glow and a last weak crackle as the charge bleeds away and no carcass.
